summary: Smart Dynamic Credit System + Economics Dashboard — comprehensive backend + frontend testing. 16/17 backend tests PASS (1 BYOK test SKIP — expected, LLM 502 with fake key; key-resolution path manually verified — OpenAI returned AuthenticationError for sk-test-******-xxx proving BYOK key was forwarded). Frontend Pricing + EconomicsDashboard pages fully render with all required testids and 6 models + BYOK column + top-up packs. Owner-only gating works correctly across backend AND frontend.

issues: 'platform margin 150%' wording. PLATFORM_MARGIN=2.5 means 2.5× markup which is 150% ABOVE cost — phrasing is technically correct but could confuse users who read it as 'we only keep 150%'. Consider 'margin 2.5×' or '60% gross margin' for clarity. Minor copy nit.

passed_tests: POST /api/credits/estimate — empty body returns 24-row matrix, models[], actions[], platform_margin=2.5, credit_value_usd=0.01, model_costs, min_credits
POST /api/credits/estimate — unknown model → 400; unknown action → 400; anonymous → 401
GET /api/admin/economics — owner 200 with full shape (window, lifetime, per_model, by_key_source, top_spenders, daily); days clamp 0→1, 9999→365
GET /api/admin/economics — dev admin (benjamin) → 403 OWNER_ONLY; freeuser → 403; anonymous → 401
POST /api/vibe/chat — dynamic billing: credits_used, balance_remaining, input_tokens>0, output_tokens>0, model, key_source='platform', cost_breakdown with all 6 sub-fields
POST /api/vibe/generate — same dynamic shape, valid token counts
POST /api/vibe/recommend-model — returns model, credits_used, balance_remaining, cost_breakdown
POST /api/armory/build-bot — dynamic billing shape with cost_breakdown.model and key_source
Race-safety: 5 concurrent /vibe/chat → balance decremented exactly by sum(credits_used). Atomicity invariant: bal0 - bal1 == credits_consumed.

Ledger metadata: verified via /api/credits/me — credit_transactions[*].metadata = {credit_calculation:'dynamic', model, input_tokens, output_tokens, api_cost_usd, revenue_usd, key_source:'platform'} written correctly by smart_credits.debit_actual_usage

BYOK path: openai key stored via POST /api/workflows/credentials → /vibe/chat with gpt-4o-mini forwards key (got OpenAI AuthenticationError for sk-test-******-xxx). Debit assertion skipped because 502 fires before debit — by design per spec.
Frontend /pricing: model-cost-matrix, byok-panel, topup-starter/-builder/-operator/-agency all present; 6 models rendered; tier grid + billing toggle visible

Frontend /admin/economics: economics-dashboard, econ-card-revenue/-cost/-margin/-users, econ-window-7/-30/-90/-365 all present; per-model table, platform-vs-BYOK split, top-spenders, daily bars all render
action_items: [OPTIONAL/COPY] Consider clarifying 'margin 150%' wording on Pricing page header and EconomicsDashboard subheader. Technically correct (2.5× = 150% markup) but easy to misread.

[INFO] BYOK debit assertion (credits_used==1, api_cost_usd==0) could not be runtime-verified because the fake key trips a 502 before debit. The key-resolution path IS verified (OpenAI returned AuthenticationError with masked sk-test key). To fully exercise the BYOK debit path, seed a valid sandbox OpenAI key OR add a 'dry-run' mode to smart_credits that runs debit even on 502.
critical_code_review_comments: smart_credits.debit_actual_usage uses find_one_and_update with sort=[('created_at',-1)] to patch metadata onto the just-inserted ledger row. This is correct but racy if two debits happen within the same microsecond for the same user — the second debit could overwrite the first's metadata. Low probability given the per-call latency, but consider matching on ref AND created_at>=just_now timestamp for extra safety.

credits_economics._require_owner correctly checks role=='admin' AND is_owner — verified via dev-admin 403 test. The HTTPException(detail={error,message}) returns a JSON detail body which FastAPI wraps as {detail:{error,message}} — frontend should extract detail.error.
Race-safety is sound: credit_wallet.debit (atomic find_one_and_update) is used as the gate. 5 concurrent calls all debited cleanly with consistent balance.
Frontend Pricing.jsx exposes margin as 'platform margin 150%' — strictly speaking 2.5× markup means 2.5x the cost; the resulting gross margin is 60%. Worth a 5-min copy review.
Ledger metadata.api_cost_usd values (~0.0001) and revenue_usd (0.01) are correct: small messages, MIN_CREDITS floor of 1cr=$0.01 dominates because raw token cost is below the floor.
test_credentials: admin@nova.ai/admin123 (owner) · benjamin@taskforce.ai/benjamin-J7VBJ4rL (dev admin) · freeuser@test.com/test123 (had 4795cr balance, sufficient for tests)
seed_data_creation: Several vibe_sessions and bot_projects created via admin@nova.ai during tests. One openai BYOK credential stored under admin@nova.ai (intentional, not cleaned — used as state for future BYOK tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend: all economics + dynamic-billing endpoints solid. Ledger metadata.* is being written correctly (verified via /api/credits/me transactions). Owner gate honors is_owner==true; dev admin (role=admin, is_owner=false) gets 403 OWNER_ONLY. Note: anti-abuse registration limit is 24h — cannot register fresh test users from this network. Use freeuser@test.com which has ~4790cr left. Race test depends on freeuser having balance; if depleted, race test will skip. BYOK runtime debit cannot be tested without a real provider sandbox key — flag for future iteration.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found — all 11 reviewer test groups passed. The PLATFORM_MARGIN=2.5 propagates from credit_calculator.py through estimate/economics/billing endpoints to the frontend matrix. Atomic debit via credit_wallet.debit prevents overspend. BYOK path correctly charges MIN_CREDITS floor (verified via code review; runtime test blocked only by fake-key 502 — by design per spec).
